page: Amazon Web Services c6id.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors. It features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes 7600 GB of bundled NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core performance, Passmark CPU score, and Passmark CPU extended instructions all ranking in the top 10% of tested servers. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small and large block operations reaching top-tier status. Single-core performance, however, is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the middle 50%. The server demonstrates strong multi-threaded decompression, ray tracing, and image processing capabilities, alongside high-throughput Redis SET operations and static web serving.

As a bare-metal instance, c6id.metal offers direct hardware access, eliminating virtualization overhead. The inclusion of 7.6 TB of NVMe SSD storage provides high resource density, making it a cost-effective option for workloads requiring high-speed local storage alongside dense compute resources. The primary tradeoff is the low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, which may limit memory-bound applications. This hardware profile is suited for multi-threaded compute workloads, database operations, static web serving, and local-storage-heavy data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c6id.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs from Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz, paired with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory read bandwidth, though single-core benchmarks remain in the average tier. With a baseline network speed of 50 Gbps and direct hardware access, this instance provides high resource density for data-intensive applications. The low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB represents a key tradeoff, positioning the server for compute-heavy workloads that benefit from high-speed local storage. It is suited for multi-threaded processing, database operations, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6id.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and 7600 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for compute and database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6id.metal features 128 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 7.6 TB NVMe SSD storage for compute-intensive bare-metal workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal compute platform combining 128 dedicated vCPUs with high-capacity local NVMe storage for intensive multi-threaded workloads.

7600 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory read bandwidth performance
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with bare-metal access
Suited for database operations, multi-threaded processing, and web serving
